description: Canonical two-call tool-use flow: the user asks an arithmetic question, the model selects the calculator tool, the agent runtime executes it and appends the result, and the model turns the observation into a final answer. Used by the P0.4 acceptance tests and, later, the flagship lesson's execution-trace visualization.

respond.text: I'll work that out with the calculator.

turns.teaching: The model does not compute — it recognizes that arithmetic is unreliable in-weights and SELECTS the calculator tool. Note the stop reason: 'tool-call' means the model is waiting for something outside itself.

The AGENT RUNTIME (not the model, not this provider) executed the tool and appended the result as a tool message. The model now consumes that observation and produces the final answer — stop reason 'completed'. Two model calls, one tool round-trip: the smallest real agent step.
